title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. corbierei
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. corbierei, also known as the Jersey kidney vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and promoting tissue repair due to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. corbierei include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine to support skin health and immune system function.
